init.description: Interview the operator and write DOMAIN.md: product, stack, known bounded contexts, the domain-expert roster, and the default lens. Read first by every other verb.

map.description: Generate a context map from intent: bounded contexts, subdomain classification (core/supporting/generic), and named, directed relationships between them.

critique.description: Read the repo and surface the de-facto context map the code actually implies versus what was intended. Flags drift and anti-patterns; every claim cites code.

language.description: Build or refine the ubiquitous language for a context; in critique mode, flag where the code's names diverge from the agreed language.

boundaries.description: Assess bounded-context boundaries and name the relationship pattern across a seam: shared kernel, customer-supplier, conformist, anti-corruption layer, open-host, partnership, separate ways.
